summary:
The primary goal during a phase I trial involves establishing initial dosing levels as well as evaluating toxicity; it prioritizes patient safety over other considerations like long-term effectiveness at this stage before moving forward into larger scale studies that can assess therapeutic benefits more comprehensively across different populations under varied conditions post-safety confirmation from phases one through three if necessary depending upon results obtained initially herein within scope defined by regulatory bodies involved globally such WHO FDA EMA etc.. Agent1 concludes emphasizing both aspects but highlights importance given priority due nature of early intervention aimed specifically towards safeguarding human subjects participating therein throughout entire process until final approval granted based comprehensive data gathered thus far including interim findings reported periodically thereafter according protocol guidelines established prior initiation thereof..
To summarize:

- Establish initial dosing levels
- Evaluate toxicity/safeguard patients

The major aim focuses on ensuring safety and determining appropriate doses without immediate emphasis on efficacy. Safety takes precedence because confirming no significant adverse reactions allows progression to later stages where treatment outcomes are assessed. [36 characters] Summary ends here. ```plaintext
Phase 1 mainly ensures safety & finds optimal dosage avoiding severe side effects.
```
